page: Microsoft Azure E48s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz and includes a 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data shows strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 250.5 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 126.78 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Most general compute metrics fall within the average range, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 13756.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 38554.05. However, single-threaded performance exhibits some bottlenecks, as evidenced by a weak bzip3 decompression rate of 8.79 GB/sec. Large language model inference also shows a clear division: prompt processing speeds are average, but text generation is weak, yielding only 7.14 tokens/sec on medium models and failing to process large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48s_v4 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage means users must provision external Premium Storage, which influences the overall deployment cost structure. This server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E48s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. While it lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results highlight strong uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths, placing in the top 25% tier. Conversely, single-threaded tasks and LLM text generation show weaker performance, with bzip3 single-threaded decompression and medium-model text generation scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It features strong large-block memory bandwidth but exhibits weaker single-threaded decompression speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and strong large-block memory bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and strong large-block memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores
384.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation
Strong uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth performance
